oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object routed by operation) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The input is rejected if the secret value is null, an empty string, the integer 0, or any object/array.
When include_transport_signal is true, the output includes the line 'verify_url=<computed-transport-url>'.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_secret_validation, feature2_service_reply_parsing, feature3_request_payload_formatting, feature4_1_token_verification_basics, feature4_2_additional_verification_checks, feature4_3_challenge_timeout_check, feature5_1_http_form_transport, feature5_2_socket_form_transport. Feature descriptions include: Reject invalid shared-secret inputs before verification is attempted.; Parse a verification service reply into success, error-code, and metadata fields.; Build the outbound verification payload fields and form-encoded query string.; Verify a user response token and return either the service outcome or a missing-token error without contacting the service.; Apply optional hostname, mobile application package, action, and score constraints after the service reply is parsed.; Reject a successful service reply when the challenge timestamp is older than the configured timeout.; Submit form-encoded verification data through an HTTP form transport and surface the raw service body or connection error body.; Submit form-encoded verification data over a direct TLS socket and parse the HTTP response body or synthesize transport error bodies.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: operation=['build_request', 'create_client', 'http_post_transport', 'parse_response', 'socket_post_transport', 'verify_token'], secret=['SECRET', 'secret', '{dict}'], service_reply=['BAD JSON', '{"success": false, "error-codes": ["test"], "hostname": "google.com", "challenge', '{"success": true}'], response=['RESPONSE', 'response'], remote_ip=['REMOTEIP', 'remoteip'], client_version=['VERSION', 'version'], user_response_token=['', 'response'], service_response=['{dict}'], expected_hostname=['host.name'], expected_apk_package_name=['apk.name'], expected_action=['action/name'], score_threshold=['0.5'], challenge_timeout_seconds=['60'], raw_reply=['False', 'RESPONSEBODY'], verify_url=['OVERRIDE', 'https://over.ride/some/path'], include_transport_signal=['True'], wire_reply=['False', 'HTTP/1.1 200 OK\n\nRESPONSEBODY', 'HTTP/1.1 500 NOPEn\\nBOBBINS']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the compact JSON serialization pattern used in headers
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Fields must be serialized as a single line compact JSON object `{"key":"value",...}` with no extra spaces, matching the headers module's format.

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the standard verbatim newline rule
context_pointers.actual_meaning:
All error blocks must start with a literal `
` and end with `
`, with fields separated by single `
` characters, avoiding auto-pretty-printing.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_secret_validation.json.description: Reject invalid shared-secret inputs before verification is attempted.

feature2_service_reply_parsing.json.description: Parse a verification service reply into success, error-code, and metadata fields.

cases.expected_output:
success=true
error_codes=[]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=null

success=false
error_codes=["test"]
hostname=google.com
challenge_ts=timestamp
apk_package_name=apk
score=0.5
action=action

success=false
error_codes=["invalid-json"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=null

feature3_request_payload_formatting.json.description: Build the outbound verification payload fields and form-encoded query string.

feature4_1_token_verification_basics.jso.description: Verify a user response token and return either the service outcome or a missing-token error without contacting the service.

cases.expected_output:
success=false
error_codes=["missing-input-response"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=null

success=true
error_codes=[]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=null

feature4_2_additional_verification_check.description: Apply optional hostname, mobile application package, action, and score constraints after the service reply is parsed.

cases.expected_output:
success=false
error_codes=["hostname-mismatch"]
hostname=host.NOTname
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=null

success=false
error_codes=["apk_package_name-mismatch"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=apk.NOTname
score=null
action=null

success=false
error_codes=["action-mismatch"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=action/NOTname

success=false
error_codes=["score-threshold-not-met"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=0.1
action=null

success=false
error_codes=["initial-error","score-threshold-not-met"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=null
apk_package_name=null
score=0.1
action=null

feature4_3_challenge_timeout_check.json.description: Reject a successful service reply when the challenge timestamp is older than the configured timeout.

cases.expected_output:
success=false
error_codes=["challenge-timeout"]
hostname=null
challenge_ts=2018-Jul-31T13:48:41Z
apk_package_name=null
score=null
action=null

feature5_1_http_form_transport.json.description: Submit form-encoded verification data through an HTTP form transport and surface the raw service body or connection error body.

feature5_2_socket_form_transport.json.description: Submit form-encoded verification data over a direct TLS socket and parse the HTTP response body or synthesize transport error bodies.

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "create_client" with secret value that is null, empty string, 0, object, or array
_constraints.output_rule: Return a single line: error=invalid_secret\n

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "parse_response" with valid success response
_constraints.output_rule: Render success=true, empty error_codes list, and null for all metadata fields

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "parse_response" with valid failure response
_constraints.output_rule: Render success=false, render error_codes list exactly as provided, render value of each metadata field if present, null if missing

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "parse_response" with invalid/empty JSON string
_constraints.output_rule: Render success=false, error_codes=["invalid-json"], and null for all metadata fields

_constraints.input_condition: Any parse response includes a numeric score field
_constraints.output_rule: Render the score as a decimal value (e.g., 0.5), not a string

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "build_request"
_constraints.output_rule: First line renders fields as compact JSON, second line renders form-encoded query string. Null/empty optional fields must be omitted entirely from both forms.

_constraints.input_condition: Field keys in request format
_constraints.output_rule: secret, response, remoteip, version

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "verify_token" with empty user_response_token
_constraints.output_rule: Render success=false, error_codes=["missing-input-response"], and null for all metadata fields

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "verify_token" with non-empty token and valid success reply
_constraints.output_rule: Render success=true and parse response fields

_constraints.input_condition: expected_hostname config exists and does not match service hostname (case-insensitive)
_constraints.output_rule: Append "hostname-mismatch" to error_codes, render service hostname

_constraints.input_condition: expected_apk_package_name config exists and does not match service apk_package_name (case-insensitive)
_constraints.output_rule: Append "apk_package_name-mismatch" to error_codes, render service apk_package_name

_constraints.input_condition: expected_action config exists and does not match service action (case-insensitive)
_constraints.output_rule: Append "action-mismatch" to error_codes, render service action

_constraints.input_condition: score_threshold config exists and service score is less than threshold
_constraints.output_rule: Append "score-threshold-not-met" to error_codes, render service score

_constraints.input_condition: Service reply returns false with existing error_codes AND score threshold not met
_constraints.output_rule: Include service error_codes followed by "score-threshold-not-met" in a single concatenated array

_constraints.input_condition: "challenge_timeout_seconds" provided and parsed challenge_ts is older than current time + timeout
_constraints.output_rule: Append "challenge-timeout" to error_codes, render challenge_ts exactly as provided

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "http_post_transport" returns connection failure
_constraints.output_rule: Render raw_reply as JSON object with success=false and error-codes=["connection-failed"]

_constraints.input_condition: "include_transport_signal": true in http_post_transport
_constraints.output_rule: Include line mentioning the verify_url used by the transport

_constraints.input_condition: "operation": "socket_post_transport" returns HTTP 200
_constraints.output_rule: Render connection details (host, port, timeout), request line, host header, and raw reply body

_constraints.input_condition: "include_transport_signal": true in socket_post_transport
_constraints.output_rule: Include lines for connection, request_line, host_header

_constraints.input_condition: Wire reply indicates non-200 status
_constraints.output_rule: Render raw_reply as JSON object with success=false and error-codes=["bad-response"]

_constraints.input_condition: Wire reply is false (connection failed)
_constraints.output_rule: Render raw_reply as JSON object with success=false and error-codes=["connection-failed"]

_constraints.input_condition: Any verification or transport error output
_constraints.output_rule: Error details must be preceeded by newline (\\n) and terminated by newline (\\n). Fields are newline separated.

_constraints.input_condition: Field not present in service reply or valid JSON
_constraints.output_rule: Default render to "null"

_constraints.input_condition: service_reply contains special characters that break JSON
_constraints.output_rule: The adapter must parse valid JSON; undefined return from BAD JSON produces "invalid-json" error. User provided strings in input are treated as literals.

_constraints.input_condition: Socket transport connection details object construction
_constraints.output_rule: Default timeout must be 30 seconds

hide.replacement: a set of hardcoded protocol identifiers defined in config.yaml
either null, empty string, 0, an object, or an array
exact_values_to_replace: "operation": "create_client" with secret value that is null, empty string, 0, object, or array
hide.oracle_response: The input is rejected if the secret value is null, an empty string, the integer 0, or any object/array.

hide.replacement: the transport signal URL derived from request headers
hide.oracle_response: When include_transport_signal is true, the output includes the line 'verify_url=<computed-transport-url>'.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: apply the standard verbatim newline rule

All error blocks must start with a literal `
` and end with `
`, with fields separated by single `
` characters, avoiding auto-pretty-printing.
